source: Ministry of Road Transport and Highways, Government of India

no_helmet.name: Riding without Helmet
no_helmet.description: Riding a two-wheeler without wearing a protective helmet
no_helmet.safety_advice: Always wear an ISI-certified helmet. It reduces the risk of head injury by 69% and death by 42%.
no_helmet.why_exists: Head injuries are the leading cause of death in two-wheeler accidents. Helmets absorb impact and protect the skull and brain.
no_helmet.repeat_consequences: Repeated violation leads to higher fines and potential licence suspension for up to 1 year. Your insurance claim may also be rejected in case of accident.

no_seatbelt.name: Driving without Seat Belt
no_seatbelt.description: Driving a motor vehicle without wearing a seat belt
no_seatbelt.safety_advice: Seat belts reduce the risk of fatal injury by 45% for front-seat passengers. Always buckle up before starting the engine.
no_seatbelt.why_exists: In a collision, an unbelted occupant can be thrown forward at the speed the vehicle was travelling, causing fatal injuries.
no_seatbelt.repeat_consequences: Repeated violations result in doubled fines and may affect your driving record during licence renewal.

overspeeding.name: Over-Speeding
overspeeding.description: Driving a vehicle beyond the prescribed speed limit
overspeeding.safety_advice: Obey speed limits. For every 1% increase in speed, there is a 4% increase in fatal crash risk. Slow down, especially near schools and hospitals.
overspeeding.why_exists: Speed is the single biggest factor in road crash fatalities. Higher speeds mean less time to react and greater impact force.
overspeeding.repeat_consequences: Licence can be suspended for up to 3 months. For commercial vehicles, permit may be revoked. Third offense may lead to permanent disqualification.

signal_jump.name: Jumping Red Light / Traffic Signal
signal_jump.description: Disobeying traffic signals or jumping a red light
signal_jump.safety_advice: Always stop at red lights. Most intersection accidents happen due to signal violations. Wait for the green — your patience saves lives.
signal_jump.why_exists: Traffic signals coordinate traffic flow and prevent collisions at intersections. Jumping signals is one of the top causes of urban road accidents.
signal_jump.repeat_consequences: Fine increases to ₹5,000 and licence may be suspended for up to 3 months.

drunk_driving.name: Drunken Driving
drunk_driving.description: Driving under the influence of alcohol or drugs (BAC exceeding 30mg per 100ml of blood)
additional_penalties: Licence suspension for at least 6 months
drunk_driving.safety_advice: NEVER drink and drive. Even small amounts of alcohol impair judgement, reaction time, and coordination. Use a cab or designate a sober driver.
drunk_driving.why_exists: Alcohol impairs brain function critical for safe driving. Drunk driving accounts for roughly 30% of all road crash fatalities in India.
drunk_driving.repeat_consequences: Imprisonment up to 2 years, fine of ₹15,000, and licence revocation for minimum 2 years. Criminal record is created.

driving_without_licence.name: Driving Without Valid Licence
driving_without_licence.description: Driving a motor vehicle without possessing a valid driving licence
driving_without_licence.safety_advice: Always carry your valid driving licence. Apply for one at your nearest RTO. Driving without proper training puts everyone at risk.
driving_without_licence.why_exists: A driving licence ensures the driver has passed tests for road rules knowledge and driving ability. Unlicensed drivers are statistically more likely to cause accidents.
driving_without_licence.repeat_consequences: Fine doubles to ₹10,000. Vehicle will be impounded. May face imprisonment up to 3 months.

driving_without_insurance.name: Driving Without Insurance
driving_without_insurance.description: Using a motor vehicle without valid third-party insurance
driving_without_insurance.safety_advice: Vehicle insurance is mandatory by law. It protects you and others financially in case of accidents. Renew before expiry.
driving_without_insurance.why_exists: Insurance provides financial protection to accident victims. Without it, victims may not receive compensation for injuries or damages.
driving_without_insurance.repeat_consequences: Fine of ₹4,000 and/or imprisonment up to 3 months. Vehicle registration may be cancelled.

driving_without_registration.name: Driving Unregistered Vehicle
driving_without_registration.description: Driving a vehicle that is not registered with the RTO
driving_without_registration.safety_advice: Ensure your vehicle is registered and the RC book is always carried. Registration ensures vehicle meets safety standards.
driving_without_registration.why_exists: Registration tracks vehicle ownership, ensures roadworthiness, and enables law enforcement. Unregistered vehicles may not meet safety standards.
driving_without_registration.repeat_consequences: Imprisonment up to 1 year and/or fine up to ₹10,000. Vehicle will be permanently seized.

dangerous_driving.name: Dangerous / Rash Driving
dangerous_driving.description: Driving in a manner dangerous to public safety, including rash and negligent driving
dangerous_driving.safety_advice: Drive defensively. Avoid sudden lane changes, tailgating, and aggressive maneuvers. Your vehicle is not a weapon — treat it with respect.
dangerous_driving.why_exists: Rash driving is one of the top killers on Indian roads. It endangers not just the driver but pedestrians, cyclists, and other road users.
dangerous_driving.repeat_consequences: Imprisonment up to 2 years, fine of ₹10,000, and licence revocation.

using_phone_while_driving.name: Using Mobile Phone While Driving
using_phone_while_driving.description: Using a handheld mobile phone or electronic device while driving
additional_penalties: Licence suspension possible for repeat offenders
using_phone_while_driving.safety_advice: Put your phone on Do Not Disturb mode while driving. If you must take a call, pull over safely. A 2-second glance at your phone at 60 km/h means driving blind for 33 meters.
using_phone_while_driving.why_exists: Mobile phone use while driving increases crash risk by 4x. It causes visual, manual, and cognitive distraction simultaneously.
using_phone_while_driving.repeat_consequences: Fine increases to ₹10,000 and licence can be suspended for 3 months.

no_parking.name: Illegal Parking
no_parking.description: Parking a vehicle in a no-parking zone or obstructing traffic
no_parking.safety_advice: Always park in designated parking areas. Illegal parking blocks roads and causes traffic congestion, increasing accident risk for everyone.
no_parking.why_exists: Illegal parking obstructs traffic flow, blocks emergency vehicles, and creates blind spots that cause accidents.
no_parking.repeat_consequences: Fine increases and vehicle may be towed at owner's expense (₹500-2,000 towing charge).

overloading_passengers.name: Overloading Passengers
overloading_passengers.description: Carrying passengers in excess of the permitted capacity
overloading_passengers.safety_advice: Never exceed the seating capacity of your vehicle. Extra passengers means less control, longer braking distance, and higher injury risk in crashes.
overloading_passengers.why_exists: Overloading affects vehicle handling, increases braking distance, and means fewer seat belts/safety features for occupants.
overloading_passengers.repeat_consequences: Fine per extra passenger plus potential permit cancellation for commercial vehicles.

overloading_goods.name: Overloading Goods Vehicle
overloading_goods.description: Loading a goods vehicle beyond its permitted carrying capacity
additional_penalties: Vehicle detained until extra load removed
overloading_goods.safety_advice: Always adhere to the load capacity printed on your RC. Overloaded trucks are 3x more likely to be involved in fatal accidents.
overloading_goods.why_exists: Overloaded vehicles damage roads, have poor braking, are prone to tipping over, and cause a disproportionate number of fatal highway accidents.
overloading_goods.repeat_consequences: Fine of ₹40,000+ and permit can be permanently cancelled. Driver and owner both held liable.

driving_without_permit.name: Driving Without Valid Permit
driving_without_permit.description: Operating a transport/commercial vehicle without a valid permit
driving_without_permit.safety_advice: Ensure your transport vehicle has a valid permit for the route you are operating on. Check permit validity regularly.
driving_without_permit.why_exists: Permits regulate commercial transport, ensure driver qualifications, route safety, and proper insurance coverage.
driving_without_permit.repeat_consequences: Vehicle permanently seized. Imprisonment up to 6 months. Permanent disqualification from holding a transport permit.

no_puc.name: No Pollution Under Control (PUC) Certificate
no_puc.description: Driving a vehicle without a valid PUC (Pollution Under Control) certificate
additional_penalties: Vehicle registration may be cancelled for repeat offenders
no_puc.safety_advice: Get your PUC certificate renewed every 6 months. It costs only ₹60-100 and ensures your vehicle isn't harming the environment.
no_puc.why_exists: Vehicle emissions contribute to air pollution, which causes respiratory diseases. PUC testing ensures vehicles meet emission standards.
no_puc.repeat_consequences: Fine of ₹2,000 and vehicle registration may be cancelled.

wrong_side_driving.name: Driving on Wrong Side
wrong_side_driving.description: Driving a vehicle on the wrong side of the road against the flow of traffic
wrong_side_driving.safety_advice: Always drive on the correct side of the road. Wrong-side driving causes head-on collisions — the deadliest type of road crash.
wrong_side_driving.why_exists: Driving on the wrong side causes head-on collisions at combined speeds, leaving virtually no chance of survival.
wrong_side_driving.repeat_consequences: Imprisonment up to 2 years and licence revocation.

underage_driving.name: Underage Driving / Juvenile Driving
underage_driving.description: Allowing or engaging in driving by a person under the legal age (18 years)
additional_penalties: Imprisonment up to 3 years for guardian
underage_driving.safety_advice: Never let minors drive any motor vehicle. They lack the maturity, training, and legal status to handle traffic situations safely.
underage_driving.why_exists: Minors lack the cognitive development and training to handle complex traffic situations. Their accidents often result in multiple casualties.
underage_driving.repeat_consequences: Same severe penalties apply. Guardian faces imprisonment up to 3 years. Vehicle registration permanently cancelled.

racing_on_public_road.name: Racing on Public Roads
racing_on_public_road.description: Participating in or organizing unauthorized motor vehicle racing on public roads
racing_on_public_road.safety_advice: Public roads are not racetracks. If you want to experience speed, visit authorized racing tracks. Illegal racing kills innocent bystanders.
racing_on_public_road.why_exists: Unauthorized racing on public roads puts all road users at extreme risk and has caused multiple mass casualty events.
racing_on_public_road.repeat_consequences: Imprisonment up to 1 year and permanent licence revocation.

not_giving_way_to_emergency.name: Not Giving Way to Emergency Vehicles
not_giving_way_to_emergency.description: Failing to give way to fire engines, ambulances, or other emergency vehicles
not_giving_way_to_emergency.safety_advice: Always pull over and give way to ambulances, fire trucks, and police vehicles with active sirens. Someone's life may depend on those seconds.
not_giving_way_to_emergency.why_exists: Every second counts in emergencies. Blocking an ambulance can directly cause a patient's death.
not_giving_way_to_emergency.repeat_consequences: Same fine of ₹10,000 applies. Licence may be suspended.

no_number_plate.name: Driving Without Number Plate / Fancy Number Plate
no_number_plate.description: Driving without a valid number plate or using a non-standard/fancy number plate
no_number_plate.safety_advice: Use only HSRP (High Security Registration Plate) format number plates. Fancy plates are illegal and hinder law enforcement.
no_number_plate.why_exists: Number plates are essential for vehicle identification, law enforcement, and tracking stolen vehicles or hit-and-run cases.
no_number_plate.repeat_consequences: Vehicle may be impounded until proper number plate is fitted.

lane_violation.name: Lane Discipline Violation
lane_violation.description: Violating lane discipline rules, improper lane changes, or driving in wrong lane
lane_violation.safety_advice: Stay in your lane and use indicators before changing lanes. Follow lane markings — they are designed to keep traffic flowing safely.
lane_violation.why_exists: Lane discipline prevents side-swipe accidents and keeps traffic flowing smoothly. Poor lane discipline is a leading cause of highway crashes.

disobeying_authority.name: Disobeying Orders of Authorities
disobeying_authority.description: Refusing to follow the orders of a police officer or traffic authority
disobeying_authority.safety_advice: Always cooperate with traffic police. If you believe a challan is wrong, pay it and contest it legally at the traffic court.
disobeying_authority.why_exists: Traffic officers manage road safety. Disobeying them creates chaos and can escalate into dangerous situations.
disobeying_authority.repeat_consequences: Fine of ₹4,000, vehicle seizure, and possible arrest.

driving_despite_disqualification.name: Driving Despite Disqualification
driving_despite_disqualification.description: Driving a motor vehicle when your licence has been suspended or revoked
driving_despite_disqualification.safety_advice: If your licence is suspended, do not drive. Use public transport until the suspension period ends and you are legally permitted to drive again.
driving_despite_disqualification.why_exists: Licence suspension is a punishment for serious violations. Driving despite it shows disregard for the law and road safety.
driving_despite_disqualification.repeat_consequences: Imprisonment up to 3 months and permanent licence revocation possible.

honking_in_silence_zone.name: Unnecessary Honking / Honking in Silence Zone
honking_in_silence_zone.description: Using horn unnecessarily or honking in designated silence zones (hospitals, schools, courts)
honking_in_silence_zone.safety_advice: Avoid unnecessary honking. Silence zones near hospitals and schools must be strictly respected. Noise pollution affects health and concentration.
honking_in_silence_zone.why_exists: Excessive honking contributes to noise pollution, causes stress, and disrupts patients in hospitals and students in schools.

not_wearing_rear_seatbelt.name: Not Wearing Rear Seat Belt
not_wearing_rear_seatbelt.description: Rear passengers not wearing seat belts in a motor vehicle
not_wearing_rear_seatbelt.safety_advice: Rear seat belt usage is now mandatory. In a crash, an unbelted rear passenger becomes a projectile that can kill front-seat occupants.
not_wearing_rear_seatbelt.why_exists: Studies show unbelted rear passengers are 3x more likely to die in a crash and can cause fatal injuries to belted front passengers.
not_wearing_rear_seatbelt.repeat_consequences: Same fine applies. Enforcement is increasing with cameras.

triple_riding.name: Triple Riding on Two-Wheeler
triple_riding.description: Carrying more than one pillion rider on a two-wheeler (except child below 4 years)
triple_riding.safety_advice: Two-wheelers are designed for a maximum of two adults. Triple riding severely affects balance, braking, and control.
triple_riding.why_exists: Overloading a two-wheeler compromises stability and increases stopping distance. Falls with multiple riders cause more severe injuries.
triple_riding.repeat_consequences: Fine doubles and licence may be suspended.
